findings.description: The memo header fields (To, From, Subject) in the PDF use a tabular left-aligned layout with clear spacing between labels and values. The HTML version presents them in a more compact inline format.
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'To:', 'From:', 'Subject:' labels with values indented/aligned at a consistent tab stop. HTML shows them as 'To: Donald R. van der Vaart, Secretary' in a more flowing format without the same visual indentation.

findings.description: The digital signature block is present in the HTML but not visible in the PDF page 1.
findings.evidence: HTML page 1 shows 'Digitally signed by LYF' with DN and Date information between the From and Subject fields, which does not appear in the PDF version of page 1.

findings.description: The HTML renders the footer information (State of North Carolina address block) between pages as a visible separator, while in the PDF it appears at the bottom of page 1 only.
findings.evidence: PDF has the footer at the bottom of page 1. HTML shows it as a divider between content sections.

findings.description: Section headings use slightly different styling. PDF uses bold Roman numerals with period (e.g., 'I.') followed by tab-indented bold title. HTML uses larger heading-style text.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'I.    History/Background' with standard bold text. HTML: 'I. History/Background' rendered as a larger heading element.

findings.description: The outline list at the end of page 1 content uses Roman numerals in both versions but the HTML renders them without the tab indentation seen in the PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF has Roman numerals with significant left indentation. HTML presents them flush left.

findings.description: The document title 'MEMORANDUM' is styled as h1 with underline, but it's not the document's true title. More importantly, there is a nested <main> element inside the outer <main>, which is a structural issue. The heading hierarchy uses h1 for 'MEMORANDUM' and h2 for sections I-IV, which is acceptable, but the h1 treatment of 'MEMORANDUM' with underline styling is a minor stylistic choice rather than a structural error.
findings.evidence:
HTML:

MEMORANDUM

followed by

I. History/Background

,

II. March 22, 2016...

, etc.

findings.description: The memorandum header information (To, From, Subject, date) and the introductory paragraph appear on both page 1 and page 2 of the PDF (page 1 is a cover/first page, page 2 repeats the same content with digital signature). The HTML includes both renditions, which faithfully reproduces the PDF but results in duplicated content.
findings.evidence: The 'On March 22, 2016, I served as meeting officer...' paragraph and the outline (I-IV) appear in the HTML corresponding to both page 1 and page 2 of the PDF. This is faithful to the source PDF which itself has this duplication.

findings.description: The attachments list in the body text (Section IV on page 5) differs slightly between the PDF page 4 description and page 5 list. In page 4, the report references 'Attachment V-Supporting Documentation' and 'Attachment VI-USPS Comment', but in page 5 (the Attachments section), the numbering is different: V is 'Written Public Comments Received' and VI is 'Supporting documentation received during public hearing'. The HTML faithfully reproduces both, so this is not an HTML error but a note about the source document's internal inconsistency.
findings.evidence: Page 4 PDF: 'Attachment V-Supporting Documentation' and 'Attachment VI-USPS Comment'; Page 5 PDF: 'V. Written Public Comments Received' and 'VI. Supporting documentation received during public hearing'
